items.text:
Rolling Oats Food Truck — Weekly Specials (June 1 to June 7)

Find us parked outside Whitman Library from 11:00 AM to 2:30 PM on weekdays, and at the Riverside Quad from 5:00 PM to 8:00 PM on Friday and Saturday. All items are made fresh each morning by Chef Daniela Ortiz, and we use local produce from the Greenfield Farmers Market.

Monday — Smoky Black Bean Bowl: brown rice, roasted peppers, lime crema. $7.50
Tuesday — Grilled Halloumi Wrap: tomato jam, arugula, herbed flatbread. $8.25
Wednesday — Korean BBQ Sliders (three per order) with pickled cucumber. $9.00
Thursday — Spicy Pumpkin Soup served with rosemary focaccia. $6.75
Friday — Lemon Herb Chicken Plate with quinoa salad and yogurt sauce. $9.50
Saturday — Sweet Potato Tacos (two per order) topped with chipotle slaw. $8.00

Add a house-made hibiscus iced tea for $2.50, or any cookie from our bakery partner for $1.75. Students who present a valid campus ID before noon receive a $1.00 discount on any plated entree. Pre-order through our website by 9:00 AM to skip the line. Cash, card, and CampusBucks accepted.

Follow @RollingOatsTruck for last-minute location changes due to weather.

format_metadata.title: Rolling Oats Food Truck — Weekly Specials

questions.stem: According to the menu, where is the truck parked on weekday afternoons?
questions.explanation: The menu states the truck parks outside Whitman Library from 11:00 AM to 2:30 PM on weekdays. Riverside Quad is the Friday/Saturday evening spot, and the farmers market is a supplier, not a location.

questions.stem: What does the menu say students can do to receive a $1.00 discount?
questions.explanation: The menu specifies that students who present a valid campus ID before noon receive a $1.00 discount on any plated entree. The other options mix real menu terms (CampusBucks, website orders, weekends) with incorrect conditions.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about pre-orders placed at 10:00 AM on a weekday?

options.B: They will not skip the line
options.D: They must be paid in cash

questions.explanation: The menu says pre-orders must be placed by 9:00 AM to skip the line, so a 10:00 AM order misses the cutoff and will not skip the line. The other choices invent benefits or restrictions not supported by the text.

items.text:
NOTICE: Spring Term Course Evaluation Deadline

Posted by the Office of Academic Assessment — Effective May 30

All undergraduate students are reminded that course evaluations for the spring term must be submitted online by 11:59 PM on Friday, June 12. Evaluations are reviewed by department chairs and are used to guide curriculum changes for the following academic year. Responses are kept anonymous; instructors will not see individual feedback until final grades have been posted.

Please follow the steps below:

- Log in to the student portal at my.campus.edu using your university ID.
- Select the "Evaluations" tab and complete one survey for each enrolled course.
- Save your progress if needed; surveys may be resumed within 14 days.
- Submit before midnight on June 12 to be entered in the bookstore gift card drawing.

Students who complete every assigned evaluation by June 5 will receive a $10.00 credit toward the campus printing account. Late submissions will not be accepted, and unfinished surveys will be discarded after the deadline passes.

For technical issues, contact the help desk at assess-help@campus.edu or visit Room 112 of Harlow Hall between 9:00 AM and 4:30 PM, Monday through Friday. Paper copies are available upon request for students with approved accommodations.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what reward is offered for finishing every evaluation by June 5?
questions.explanation: The notice states that students who complete every assigned evaluation by June 5 will receive a $10.00 credit toward the campus printing account. The gift card drawing is a separate June 12 incentive.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a student who submits a survey on June 13?
questions.explanation: The notice says late submissions will not be accepted and that unfinished surveys will be discarded after the deadline. Since June 13 is past the June 12 deadline, the response will be discarded.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of the notice?

options.B: To announce a new grading policy

questions.explanation: The notice primarily informs students about the course evaluation deadline, steps, and incentives. The other choices touch on minor details (help desk, curriculum review) but are not the main purpose.
